g.data-narration: A destructor is a special member function that cleans up when objects are destroyed, releasing memory and resources.
g.text: Special member function that performs cleanup when object is destroyed
• Same name as class with ~ prefix

g.data-narration: Destructors prevent memory leaks by releasing dynamically allocated memory, file handles, and other system resources.
g.text: Without proper destructors, programs suffer from:

g.data-narration: Key characteristics: no return type, cannot be overloaded, automatically called when objects go out of scope.
In inheritance, destructors execute in reverse order of construction: derived first, then base class.
g.text: Destructors execute in REVERSE order of construction

g.data-narration: Without virtual destructors, deleting a derived object through a base pointer causes memory leaks - only base destructor runs.
g.text: When base pointer deletes derived object - only BASE destructor called!

g.data-narration: Virtual destructors solve this by ensuring both derived and base destructors execute in correct reverse order.
g.text: Declare base destructor as virtual - ensures proper cleanup of derived objects

g.data-narration: Pure virtual destructors create abstract classes but require an implementation - unlike regular pure virtual functions.
g.text: Creates abstract base class but MUST provide implementation

g.data-narration: In this example, SportsCar constructor runs first for the most derived class, and destructor runs last for cleanup.
g.text: Multiple levels of inheritance - observe construction and destruction order

g.data-narration: This example shows Animal base class and Dog derived class - without virtual destructor, Dog's memory is leaked.
g.text: Animal base class with Dog derived class containing dynamic memory

g.data-narration: Key rules: always use virtual in polymorphic base classes, not needed for non-polymorphic classes, adds vtable overhead.
Key points: destructors cannot be overloaded, execute in reverse order of construction, must be virtual for polymorphic deletion.
g.text: • Destructors: No return type, no params, cannot be overloaded
• Order: Constructors run base→derived, Destructors run derived→base
• Virtual: Required for polymorphic deletion through base pointers
• Auto: Invoked on scope exit or delete

g.data-narration: Remember: virtual for delete rule, visualize construction/destruction order, practice identifying memory leaks in code.
